items.title: RT Danny (Dennis) Citrinowicz ,داني سيترينوفيتش: The choice in the coming days could not be clearer: either an American willingness to ...
items.description_html:
RT Danny (Dennis) Citrinowicz ,داني سيترينوفيتش
The choice in the coming days could not be clearer: either an American willingness to pursue negotiations that address at least some of Iran’s core demands, or a return to military confrontation. A course that may achieve tactical gains, but is unlikely to force Iranian capitulation.

We have once again reached a decisive moment. Yet the continued exchange of messages over recent days suggests that, alongside preparations for escalation, there remains an effort to find a diplomatic pathway and bridge the deep gaps between the two sides.

Above all, Trump faces a fundamental strategic decision. He cannot simultaneously frame Iran as a regime to be destroyed while also expecting meaningful negotiations to succeed.

If he chooses the diplomatic track and is prepared to offer Tehran some degree of economic relief or concessions, there is still a path toward an agreement. But Iran is unlikely to abandon the ideological pillars that define the regime and underpin its regional posture. If Trump assumes otherwise, he is likely to face disappointment once again whether he will choose diplomacy or kinetics. As long as this regime in power - capitulation is not an option for them.

It is what it is...

#IranWar‌


Gregory Brew: FWIW Iran's proposal last weekend went very far, even a slight revision would be an improvement. It's easy to see what Trump wants, harder to see what, exactly, will drive him to resume bombing (which, to be sure, he could decide to do at any time).

Exports to China fell for the 11th consecutive month, with China’s share of U.S. exports dropping to 3.8%. Exports to Taiwan and India rose YoY.

items.title: BREAKING: Credit card serious delinquencies rose +0.4 percentage points in Q1 2026, to 13.1%, the highest since Q4 2010. This is only below the 2010 p...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Credit card serious delinquencies rose +0.4 percentage points in Q1 2026, to 13.1%, the highest since Q4 2010.

This is only below the 2010 peak of 13.7%, in the aftermath of the 2008 Financial Crisis.

Since Q3 2022, serious credit card delinquencies have surged +5.5 percentage points, even larger than the +3.9 point increase in 2007-2010.

Furthermore, student loan 90+ days delinquencies jumped +0.7 percentage points in Q1 2026, to 10.3%, the highest since Q1 2020.

Auto loan serious delinquencies increased +0.4 percentage points, to 5.6%, the highest on record.

US consumers are falling behind debt at a crisis pace.

items.title: Risk appetite in South Korea is exploding: Margin loans outstanding on Korean stocks are up to a record $24.3 billion. Since the start of 2025, margin...
items.description_html:
Risk appetite in South Korea is exploding:

Margin loans outstanding on Korean stocks are up to a record $24.3 billion.

Since the start of 2025, margin debt has surged +140% and is up +32% since the beginning of this year alone.

To put this into perspective, the value of leveraged bets on Korean stocks was ~$5.0 billion in 2020.

This also likely understates the real scale, given that many loans taken out to buy stocks are labeled under other categories.

Meanwhile, domestic investors have poured ~$25.3 billion into South Korean shares year-to-date.

Retail investors in South Korea are aggressively rushing into stocks.

items.title: BREAKING: The SEC is set to release its so-called "innovation exemption" for tokenized stocks which will pave the path for trading digital versions of...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The SEC is set to release its so-called "innovation exemption" for tokenized stocks which will pave the path for trading digital versions of securities, per Bloomberg.

Details include:

1. In a "surprise move," the SEC is leaning toward allowing the trading of tokenized assets

2. These tokenized assets would be tradeable on decentralized crypto platforms

3. The move could "reshape the landscape of the American stock market"

4. This would also be one of the US' biggest shifts into crypto infrastructure yet

Tokenized assets are rapidly expanding.

items.title: Shocking stat of the day: Semiconductor stocks have accounted for more than half of the S&P 500's +8% year-to-date gain, or +563 index points. NVIDIA,...
items.description_html:
Shocking stat of the day:

Semiconductor stocks have accounted for more than half of the S&P 500's +8% year-to-date gain, or +563 index points.

NVIDIA, $NVDA, alone has contributed +110 index points to the S&P 500.

This is followed by Micron, $MU, at +58 points, Broadcom, $AVGO, at +44 points, AMD, $AMD, at +40 points, and Intel, $INTC, at +39 points.

As a result, the remaining 495 stocks in the index have collectively contributed +272 points.

This comes as the Semiconductor index, $SOX, has rallied +64%, 8x the performance of the S&P 500.

Chip stocks now also account for 18% of the S&P 500’s market cap, near an all-time high.

Semiconductor stocks are a modern-day gold rush.

items.title: Investor risk appetite is surging at a historic pace: The ratio of levered long to short ETF trading volume is up to ~3.3, the highest since July 2024...
items.description_html:
Investor risk appetite is surging at a historic pace:

The ratio of levered long to short ETF trading volume is up to ~3.3, the highest since July 2024.

In other words, trading activity in levered long ETFs is more than 3 times larger than that of leveraged short ETFs.

This ratio has more than DOUBLED since late March and is comparable to the peak level seen during the 2020 market recovery following the pandemic crash.

The only other period that significantly exceeded the current level is the 2021 meme stock frenzy, when the indicator jumped to ~6.0.

Meanwhile, assets under management in US leveraged ETFs is up to a record $180 billion, rising ~$75 billion over the last 12 months.

Investors are extremely bullish right now.

items.title: BREAKING: Iran has sent an updated proposal for a deal to end the war, but the White House believes it is "insufficient for a deal," per Axios. Presid...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Iran has sent an updated proposal for a deal to end the war, but the White House believes it is "insufficient for a deal," per Axios.

President Trump is now expected to convene his top national security team in the Situation Room on Tuesday to discuss military options.

A senior US official said that if Iran will not shift its position, the US will have to continue negotiations "through bombs."

Trump is reportedly considering resuming the Iran War due to Iran's rejection of many of his demands.

Peace talks continue to show little progress.

items.title: BREAKING: US CPI inflation is on track to exceed +5.0% as early as this year. Over the last 6 months, CPI inflation has averaged +0.4% on a MoM basis,...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: US CPI inflation is on track to exceed +5.0% as early as this year.

Over the last 6 months, CPI inflation has averaged +0.4% on a MoM basis, with March and April readings as high as +0.9% and +0.6%, respectively.

If this trend continues, this puts YoY inflation on pace to surge to +5.2% by the November midterms.

That would be the highest level since February 2023 and more than double the February 2026 print.

Even if monthly inflation prints ease to +0.3%, the YoY inflation rate would still rise to +4.4%, the highest since April 2023.

Inflation is back in full swing.

items.title: Hedge funds are all-in on semiconductor stocks: Semiconductor and semiconductor equipment stocks now account for 19% of total global hedge fund market...
items.description_html:
Hedge funds are all-in on semiconductor stocks:

Semiconductor and semiconductor equipment stocks now account for 19% of total global hedge fund market exposure, the highest on record.

This percentage has more than DOUBLED since the start of 2026.

By comparison, semiconductor stocks represented less than 2% of global hedge fund exposure during the 2022 bear market.

This comes as the Semiconductor index, $SOX, has rallied +405% over this period.

To put this into perspective, software and services stocks now account for ~2% of hedge fund portfolios.

This metric has declined -10 percentage points over the last 4 years.

Hedge funds are extremely bullish on chip stocks.

items.title: BREAKING: Iran has launched "Hormuz Safe," a Bitcoin-backed insurance service for shipping companies that want to transit the Strait of Hormuz. Detail...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Iran has launched "Hormuz Safe," a Bitcoin-backed insurance service for shipping companies that want to transit the Strait of Hormuz.

Details include:

1. The Iranian government says it could generate more than $10 billion in revenue from the program

2. The service will be for "Iranian shipping companies and cargo owners"

3. "The shipment will be covered from the moment of confirmation and signed receipt will be given to the owner," Iran says

4. It is unclear if this insurance service will be charged in addition to tolls, which have been as much as $2 million per ship

Iran says an official website with more information is "coming soon."

items.title: BREAKING: The White House announces multiple US-China trade deals and developments following President Trump's meeting with China's President Xi. Deta...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The White House announces multiple US-China trade deals and developments following President Trump's meeting with China's President Xi.

Details include:

1. China will address US concerns regarding supply chain shortages related to rare earths and other critical minerals

2. China has approved an initial purchase of 200 American-made Boeing aircraft for Chinese airlines

3. China will purchase at least $17 billion per year of US agricultural products in 2026, 2027, and 2028

4. China has restored market access for US beef by renewing expired listings of more than 400 US beef facilities and adding new listings

5. China has resumed imports of poultry from US states determined by the USDA to be free of highly pathogenic avian influenza

President Xi will be visiting the White House this fall.

items.title: BREAKING: The White House announces the establishment of the US-China Boards of Trade and Investment to "optimize the bilateral economic relationship"...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The White House announces the establishment of the US-China Boards of Trade and Investment to "optimize the bilateral economic relationship" between China and the US.

The Board of Trade will allow the US Government and China to manage bilateral trade "across non-sensitive goods."

The Board of Investment will provide a government-to-government forum for discussing investment-related issues.

This announcement comes just days after the US-China summit.

items.title: It simply won't stop. It is Sunday night and the US 10Y Note Yield just casually hit 4.63%, the highest since February 2025. We are now ~4 basis point...
items.description_html:
It simply won't stop.

It is Sunday night and the US 10Y Note Yield just casually hit 4.63%, the highest since February 2025.

We are now ~4 basis points ABOVE the high that prompted President Trump's "90-day tariff pause" in April 2025.

This puts the 10Y Note Yield up +70 basis points since the Iran War, with US mortgage rates now nearing 7.00%+.

And, in a sudden turn of events, the odds of rate cuts have collapsed to 2% this year and US inflation is nearing 4%+.

The US bond market is collapsing in real-time.

items.title: Americans are defaulting on their student loan debt at a record pace: Delinquent federal student loan debt jumped +$12.2 billion in Q1 2026, to $171.4...
items.description_html:
Americans are defaulting on their student loan debt at a record pace:

Delinquent federal student loan debt jumped +$12.2 billion in Q1 2026, to $171.4 billion, an all-time high.

This has officially surpassed the $166.8 billion peak recorded in Q4 2019.

At the same time, the proportion of seriously delinquent loans rose +0.7 percentage points, to 10.3%, the highest since Q1 2020.

This comes as 2.6 million borrowers defaulted in Q1 2026, followed by ~1.0 million in Q4 2025.

The average borrower entering default is now nearly 40 years old, up from 36.4 before the 2020 pandemic.

The US student loan crisis is intensifying.

items.title: RT Robert Sterling: I just had the craziest experience at the airport. We are about to board a flight to Atlanta when the pilot from the incoming plan...
items.description_html:
RT Robert Sterling
I just had the craziest experience at the airport.

We are about to board a flight to Atlanta when the pilot from the incoming plane walks out of the jetway. Guy is probably late 50s, salt and pepper hair, military look. The kind of pilot you instantly feel good about seeing on your flight.

Pilot walks over to the counter, gets on the PA system, and starts addressing everyone. “Folks, I’ve been doing this a long time. Flying one of these jets is easy. The hard part is looking at 130 people and telling them their flight is going to be delayed.”

Audible groans throughout the boarding gate. Most people here are flying to Atlanta as a layover before another flight. 130 people just had their day become a complete mess.

The pilot goes on. “I get it, trust me. But here’s the deal: During our landing, we had a small mechanical issue. I’m not your pilot for the next leg, but I don’t feel confident the jet’s safe to fly until we have a mechanical team look it over, and I don’t feel comfortable asking the next pilots to fly you guys until we get confirmation.”

He points at the agents next to him behind the counter: “Now, none of this is the agents’ fault. Please be kind to them. I’m the one who made this decision, not them, so any inconvenience you experience is my fault. Just please know that I don’t do this lightly, and I’m only doing it because I believe it’s in the best interests of everyone’s safety.”

Now this is where the story gets crazy. The pilot puts the microphone down, grabs his suitcase, and all the people in the gate…

Start clapping.

I’m not joking, everyone starts clapping for the guy. 130 people who just had their travel plans ruined give an ovation to the guy who made the decision and delivered the message.

All because he addressed them with decency and transparency, took ownership of the decision, made it clear that it was necessary, and explained why it was in everyone’s best interest.

It’s honestly one of the best examples of strong communication—of strong leadership, for that matter—that I’ve seen in a long time.

@Delta, whoever your Atlanta to Wichita pilot was this morning, he’s one of the good ones. Please tell him the delayed passengers of flight 1637 appreciate what he did.

items.title: RT Acyn: Trump on Taiwan: When you look at the odds, China is very, very powerful, big country. That's a very small island. Think of it, it's 59 miles...
items.description_html:
RT Acyn
Trump on Taiwan: When you look at the odds, China is very, very powerful, big country. That's a very small island. Think of it, it's 59 miles away. We're 9500 miles away. That's a little bit of a difficult problem. Taiwan was developed because we had presidents that didn't know what the hell they were doing. They stole our chip industry.

items.description_html:
Promoter holding of 80% sounds like conviction.

But if the promoter holds 80% and institutions hold 10%, only 10% of shares are setting the price.

More promoter = more volatility, not less.

The sweet spot is 35 to 65%. Most screeners do not flag this.

items.title: RT CMO Maharashtra: 🤝CM Devendra Fadnavis presided over the signing and exchange of MoUs between the Government of Maharashtra (Energy Department) ...
items.description_html:
RT CMO Maharashtra
🤝CM Devendra Fadnavis presided over the signing and exchange of MoUs between the Government of Maharashtra (Energy Department) and various companies in Mantralaya, Mumbai, today.

Details of the MOUs:

🔸Adani Power Limited and its Coastal-Maha Atomic Energy Limited

For setting up a nuclear power plant at Barsu, Dist. Ratnagiri.
✅Proposed Capacity: 6000 MW
✅Proposed Investment: ₹1,50,000 Crore
✅Direct + Indirect Employment Opportunities: 4500 + 7500 = 12,000

🔸Reliance Industries Ltd.

For setting up Bharat Small Reactors/Small Modular Reactor (SMR)/Nuclear Power Reactors at Purnagadh, Dist. Ratnagiri.
✅Proposed Capacity: 2 X 220 MW to 6 X 1200 MW
✅Proposed Investment: ₹8000 to ₹2,00,000 Crore
✅Direct + Indirect Employment Opportunities: 3000 + 1,00,000 = 1,03,000

🔸NTPC (National Thermal Power Corporation) Ltd.

For setting up a nuclear power plant at Devgad, Dist. Sindhudurg.
✅Proposed Capacity: 6 Units/ 4000 MW - 7200 MW
✅Proposed Investment: ₹1,00,000 Crore
✅Direct + Indirect Employment Opportunities: 5000

Minister Atul Save, Business Head, Nuclear Energy at Adani Group, Vivek Sharma, President Nuclear Energy Business Reliance Industries Ltd., Ranjay Sharan, CEO, NTPC, Arnada Prasad Samal and other senior officials were present.

🤝 मुख्यमंत्री देवेंद्र फडणवीस यांच्या प्रमुख उपस्थितीत आज मंत्रालय, मुंबई येथे महाराष्ट्र शासन (ऊर्जा विभाग) आणि विविध कंपन्यांमध्ये सामंजस्य करार करण्यात आले.

सामंजस्य करारांचे तपशील

🔸अदानी पॉवर लिमिटेड व त्यांची कोस्टल-महा ॲटोमिक एनर्जी लिमिटेड

बारसू, जि. रत्नागिरी येथे अणुऊर्जा प्रकल्प उभारण्यासाठी सामंजस्य करार.
✅ प्रस्तावित स्थापित क्षमता - 6000 मेगावॅट
✅ प्रस्तावित गुंतवणूक - ₹1,50,000 कोटी
✅ प्रत्यक्ष+अप्रत्यक्ष रोजगार संधी : 4500 + 7500 = 12,000

🔸 रिलायन्स इंडस्ट्रीज लिमिटेड

पूर्णगड जि. रत्नागिरी येथे 'भारत स्मॉल रिॲक्टर्स' / स्मॉल मॉड्युलर रिॲक्टर (SMR)/ अणुऊर्जा रिॲक्टर प्रकल्प उभारण्यासाठी सामंजस्य करार.
✅ प्रस्तावित स्थापित क्षमता : 2 X 220 MW ते 6 X 1200 MW
✅ प्रस्तावित गुंतवणूक : ₹8000 ते ₹2,00,000 कोटी
✅ प्रत्यक्ष+अप्रत्यक्ष रोजगार संधी : 3000 + 1,00,000 = 1,03,000

🔸 नॅशनल थर्मल पॉवर कॉर्पोरेशन लिमिटेड (NTPC)

सिंधुदुर्ग जिल्ह्यात देवगड येथे अणुऊर्जा प्रकल्प उभारण्यासाठी सामंजस्य करार.
✅ प्रस्तावित स्थापित क्षमता : एकूण 6 युनिट्सद्वारे 4000 ते 7200 मेगावॅट
✅ प्रस्तावित गुंतवणूक : ₹1,00,000 कोटी
✅ प्रत्यक्ष+अप्रत्यक्ष रोजगार संधी : 5000

यावेळी मंत्री अतुल सावे, अदानी समूहाच्या न्यूक्लिअर एनर्जीचे बिझनेस हेड विवेक शर्मा, रिलायन्स इंडस्ट्रीज लिमिटेडच्या न्यूक्लिअर एनर्जी बिझनेसचे अध्यक्ष, रंजय शरण, एनटीपीसीचे मुख्य कार्यकारी अधिकारी अर्णदा प्रसाद सामल आणि वरिष्ठ अधिकारी उपस्थित होते.

@Dev_Fadnavis @save_atul @Adani_Elec_Mum @RIL_Updates @ntpclimited @mahaurja
#Maharashtra #DevendraFadnavis #Mumbai #NuclearEnergyAgreement

Major Governance Issues highlighted by Indusind Bank in Earnings Release

1. They are considering misclassification of certain interest income and fee income worth 800 crore as FRAUD

2. The bank has fully utilised its contingency provision, worth 2522 crore

4. Misclassification of more financial items which are wrongly considered other income as interest income and other operating expenses as provision; thus, inflating operational profitability of the bank
5. MFI biz's incorrect statement of loans and interest leading to an adverse impact of 1960 cr

6. Derivative accounting discrepancy worth 1960 crore

7. The auditor raises the concern on internal controls and the legal implications of these

Hence, Bank reported loss of 2328 crore against Bloomberg estimates of 200-250 crore
#Induisndbank #redflag #corporategovernance

items.title: Investors lost almost 99% of their wealth in BCG and Varanium. So some thoughts on mistakes...! Investing Lessons from Fraud at Brightcom & Varanium C...
items.description_html:
Investors lost almost 99% of their wealth in BCG and Varanium. So some thoughts on mistakes...!

Investing Lessons from Fraud at Brightcom & Varanium Cloud

1. Caution on small companies making big promises on hot new biz (AI, ML etc) where they don't have experience and resources.

2. Stocks are discussed too much on social media by a particular set of people might have an interest in the stock that's why you are seeing one-sided updates (only positive) of the company

3. Don't just buy because some big investor has bought (his/ her allocation could be less than 1% and your allocation going beyond 40% -60% is scary). They can also go wrong.

4. Low PE does not mean its undervalued.

5. Doing biz or most biz via foreign subsidiaries is the big problem. BCG have zero focus on India but for its peers India is big market. How can media tech company India could not be big market?? does not make sense

6. Don't fall for it just because Index Funds are buying.

7. Don't believe blindly what everything management is saying. BCG lied about its management in Investor PPt itself.

8. Try to connect dots.. if so many negatives or fishy things better to avoid them.

9. Don't catch falling knife

10. Explosive Growth in P&L without underlying growth in Cash Flows and BS is another disaster.

11. Frequent resignations in KMPs and Auditors is another recipe for disaster.

12. Selling visions and guidance via press releases does not give confidence.

13. Signing MoUs, and deals with unknown companies might be fancy but just on paper.

14. Promoters are selling, just accept it. Don't try to neglect other stories like fundraising ki vajah se holding kam hua ya SHP mai error hai.

15. Background of KMPs if looks fishy avoid it.

16. The degree and the impact of governance issues needs to be check on overall business and fundamentals. Can ignore if small ...

17. Rise of retail participation in a stock could be another sign of storified stock

18. Asset Allocation is very important, don't buy the multi-bagger stocks on day 1. Mutlibagger Stocks takes years to create Multibagger Wealth...

Kindly maximise the reach of this tweet to save investors ....!

Remember two golden rules

A. Capital Protection is much more important than Capital Appreciation
B. Read Business, Not Stock Prices

items.title: In Bull Markets storytelling becomes very common... Every stock/company tells a fascinating story about their future... But only in a bear market - we...
items.description_html:
In Bull Markets storytelling becomes very common... Every stock/company tells a fascinating story about their future...

But only in a bear market - we find which companies were actually a Storified Stock (basically which never did walk the talk)

There is another way to verify whether management is reasonable / honest or not -

Read con-calls - Cross-verify management commentaries with peers or co-relate operational aspects with financial aspect & vice versa

Let's take an example - was reading concall of a steel pipe manufacturer - JTL Industries

JTL on dealer network expansion plans and opportunity size said they have already reached saturation levels as they have around 800 dealers out of total 1100 dealers in India

To verify this whether 800 dealers is saturated number or not? I checked distributors of industry leader - APL
and I found that APL too had 800 around dealers... Hence the commentary of JTL is verified

So there are the few ways along with example of doing Walk The Talk Test of The Management

Disc - No recommendations

Some Previous Disclosure Lapses by Promoter Ashwani Khemka -
In FY13, Promoter increased stake from 7.95% to 10.10% and disclosed the same with delay of 3,227 days
Similarly, in FY 2016, Promoter sold 11.02% stake via revocation by pledge and disclosed the same with delay of 1783 days.

In the last Credit Rating Report also Crisil issued CRISIL D rating due to non-cooperation by company. Company is not sharing any data since 2018, from there in next 5 years, CRISIL issued them with CRISIL D rating.

Custom Duty worth 1.36 Cr belongs to 2020-21 (Equals to their FY21 PAT)
NMMC Cess worth 50L is payable worth 50L belongs to FY02 to FY13.

Company also has defaulted on Loan, that loan assigned to ARC which company settled.

items.title: Varanium Cloud - Saga of - Capital Misallocation | IPO Fund Mis-utilization | Discrepancy in Cash Flows | Dividend Saga 1. The company raised 40 crore...
items.description_html:
Varanium Cloud - Saga of - Capital Misallocation | IPO Fund Mis-utilization | Discrepancy in Cash Flows | Dividend Saga

1. The company raised 40 crore via IPO in Sept-22 and as of March-23, it has unutilized 15 crore from IPO funds
Now again company raised whopping 1250 crores for purpose of some acquisition (not announced yet)

2. Company has given dividend 4 times leading to outgo of INR 16 crore interestingly company has not paid any dividend before IPO.
3. Out of this 3 crores has been paid by March-23 still, there is no record of such payout in Cash Flow Statement (in Lakhs)
4. Company has negligible cash flows from other means and has negative cash flow from operations, so basically they don't have any other cash sources to pay such high amount of dividend apart from utilizing IPO funds. A possible case of IPO fund mis-utilization?

Following remain questions:
1. Why company is regularly paying dividend at one side and raising money at other side?
2. Why company wants to raise fund if IPO fund remain unutilized?
3. Why dividend paid till March-23 is not reflecting in Cash flows?
4. How company funding its huge dividend payout?

#redflag #corporategovernance #varaniumcloud #varanium

items.title: The most interesting thing in whole Reliance Retail Saga is here👇 Everyone's buzzing about Reliance paying a fancy premium on the valuation done by...
items.description_html:
The most interesting thing in whole Reliance Retail Saga is here👇

Everyone's buzzing about Reliance paying a fancy premium on the valuation done by BDO and E&Y.

But guess what?

Let me tell u sneaky tax twist too!

When a company having accumulated profits, and reduces its capital. It will be considered as Deemed Dividend and taxed like a dividend section 2(22)(d), as per the slab rate.
Yikes! 😬 But wait!

Reliance isn't one to back down. They're cleverly compensating for it by paying that premium!
Let's crunch the numbers: 1362 x 0.65 (considering 35% Tax)= 885! That's the magic valuation by E&Y!

If Reliance didn't pay the premium, shareholders would be left with just - 885 x 0.65 = 575 in their bank accounts! 😱🤯

Coming up with full story and detailed Thread on Reliance Retail. 🔍
Stay tuned 🍿

@deepakshenoy @govindethiraj
#Reliance #RelianceRetail

items.title: Investing Advice that has changed my perspective & paid off massively Read concalls daily, have weekly targets (at least 10) Explore new sectors regul...
items.description_html:
Investing Advice that has changed my perspective & paid off massively

Read concalls daily, have weekly targets (at least 10)

Explore new sectors regularly

For Eg:
If you want to learn about the PEB sector -:
Read concalls of Interarch Building & Pennar Industries

For Co-Working -: AWFIS, EFC, Smartworks, Indiqube

For NBFCs -: Bajaj Finance, SBFC, CreditAccess, Arman Financial

For Asset & Wealth Management -: Nuvama, 360One, HDFC AMC, Anand Rathi

To get more context about a company, going through the last few Investor PPTs & concalls helps a lot

Imagine this: Every quarter, companies explain their business model, future outlook & highlights directly to investors (Everything for free, yet we don't value it).
You even get a chance to ask your questions

One concall is approx 20 pages, takes around 20-30 mins to read, recording can be heard (almost 1 hr). If you increase the playback speed you can complete it in less than 30 mins

In the beginning, it may take some time, but it is worth it
As you gain experience, efficiency will increase, and it will not take more than 20-25 mins

You will see your knowledge compound in a year, and changes will be visible in a matter of weeks

I can bet you will find many multibaggers this way

Investing is all about reading & connecting the dots

items.title: Interarch Building ~ One of the best concalls post listing🔥 Management exuded strong confidence, calling themselves the "Mercedes of the PEB indust...
items.description_html:
Interarch Building ~ One of the best concalls post listing🔥

Management exuded strong confidence, calling themselves the "Mercedes of the PEB industry" with focus on quality, service, & execution

Achieved 10X growth during the 2004–2009 cycle

Confident of surpassing FY26 REV growth guidance of 17.5%

Long term aspiration to reach 2,500 Cr REV by FY28 (implying 20% CAGR) with EBITDA margins moving towards double digits

Strong moat built through 25+ years of client relationships in pre-engineered buildings, seen as difficult to replicate by competitors

Demand environment currently supported by Greenfield projects & new age sectors like Renewables, EV, Data centers, Semiconductors, Lithium-ion batteries

Capacity expansion underway with new plants in Andhra & Gujarat, Set to double capacity within 2–3 years – first major addition in 15 years

Structural runway for growth highlighted by China’s per capita steel consumption being ~10x India and global average ~5x India

Large opportunity ahead with rising steel intensity in construction across data centers, malls, housing, airports, railway stations

Management also shared competitive landscape views, inventory management, export opportunity (highly recommend going through the concall)

PEB story playing out very well, already 2X from initial coverage still lot of potential, keep tracking🔥

Lot of companies delivered blockbuster earnings🔥
Lot of Action in Mid/SmallCap💹

Strong Results:
- Financials - Housing Finance, Select NBFC's
- Few Auto Ancillaries (Lumax, Lumax Auto, Gabriel, Fiem...)

- Capital Markets (Exchanges, MFD, Asset & Wealth M)
- Recycling & few in Water Treatment (Va Tech, Enviro Infra..)
- Capital Goods (PEB, Turbines & Generator, Industrial Gears & Equipment's, Compressors etc)

- EMS

- Renewables, EPC (Cashflow concerns)

- Defence
- Aviation & Hotels
- Telecom
- Hospitals
- Life Insurance
- Sugar
- Alcoholic Beverages

Weak/Mixed Results:
- Textiles
- Banks (NIM's compression)
- Dairy
- Cement
- Building Materials
- LargeCap IT, Midcap IT (Ex Persistent, Coforge)
- Paints
- QSR (Muted SSSG)
- Paper

Keep tracking lot of bottoms up opportunity

1. Read concalls daily (atleast 2)

2. Explore new sectors regularly

items.title: Mega Wealth Creator in the Last 4 Years Cheat Code for Multibagger: - Starting Valuations: 10-20 P/E Lower the valuations, higher the upside - Revenue...
items.description_html:
Mega Wealth Creator in the Last 4 Years

Cheat Code for Multibagger:

- Starting Valuations: 10-20 P/E
Lower the valuations, higher the upside

- Revenue Growth: 20-25%
This means 2x revenue in 3-4 years

- Levers for Margin Expansion
Operating leverage must play out
PAT growth > Revenue growth

If REV doubles, PAT must increase by at least 3-5x

Observe the pattern
Do not overpay
If starting valuations are higher, the upside gets capped

The best time to add stocks is during panic situations
Always be prepared with a watchlist of stocks
In a correction of 20-25%, a 25 P/E stock comes down below 20x, that's the opportunity
Always be ready

Have a portfolio of at least 20 stocks across 10 sectors for adequate diversification

Trying to follow the same strategy—easier said than done ;)

Add only those stocks which fit this framework.
Read concalls, investor presentations, management interviews & keep tracking every quarter

One multibagger with the right allocation can change your life
Bookmark it

#investing #stockmarkets

items.description_html:
Best Investing Advice Ever ~

Read 3-4 concalls every day

Explore new sectors daily

For Eg
If you want to learn about Crane Sector
~ Read concalls of Sanghvi, Tara Chand, Ace Construction

One concall is approx 20 pages and will take around 30 mins

This adds up to about 2-3 hours daily
Assuming you made some notes as well

In the beginning, it may take some time, but it is worth it

As you gain experience, your efficiency will increase

You will see your knowledge compound in a year

I can bet you will find many multibaggers this way

Keep connecting the dots
You will gain new insights, trends

You will be ahead of many others

Investing is all about reading..

#investing #stockmarkets

items.title: How P/E Expansion Works💹 Understand this for spotting future multibaggers💸 This is the main reason why stocks give multibagger returns:🔖👇 ...
items.description_html:
How P/E Expansion Works💹

Understand this for spotting future multibaggers💸

This is the main reason why stocks give multibagger returns:🔖👇

P/E Ratio = Share Price / EPS
Initial
- Share Price - 100
- P/E - 10
- EPS - 10

1⃣Now imagine P/E expanding to 20, EPS doubling in the next 3 years:

After 3 Years
- P/E - 20
- EPS - 20
- Share Price - 400
- Returns - 4X

2⃣Now imagine P/E expanding to 100, EPS doubling in the next 3 years:

After 3 Years
- P/E - 100
- EPS - 20
- Share Price - 2000
- Returns - 20X

3⃣Now imagine earnings expanding by 5X, Exit P/E - 100:

After 3 Years
- EPS - 50
- P/E - 100
- Share Price - 5000
- Returns - 50X

This is how to generate multibagger returns.
Understand and apply the maths.
If you have found a multibagger, just keep riding.

P/E expansion happens in a bull market.
P/E contraction happens in a bear market.

It's very painful to handle a bear market.
Make as much money as you can in a bull market.
Once the bear market comes, the portfolio will be down by 40-60 %.

items.title: Semiconductors The Indian Semiconductor market is expected to grow 20-25 % over the next 10 years. Allied sectors will benefit from the semiconductor ...
items.description_html:
Semiconductors

The Indian Semiconductor market is expected to grow 20-25 % over the next 10 years.

Allied sectors will benefit from the semiconductor ecosystem.

The first major fab in India is being set up by Tata Electronics in Gujarat.

Key companies in this area include Kaynes Technology, CG Power, HCL Tech, SPEL Semiconductor.

Semiconductor manufacturing processes require high-purity gases and water, which are the second-biggest material expenditure after silicon.

Linde India, Everest Kanto, Aeroflex Industries, Inox India, and VA Tech Wabag could be the key suppliers to semiconductor companies.

Valuations must be checked independently; most of them are very expensive.

Source: MoneyControl

items.title: Cheat Code for Multi bagger :- - Starting valuations: 15-25 P/E Lower the valuations higher the upside. - Revenue growth: At least 25% This means 2x r...
items.description_html:
Cheat Code for Multi bagger :-

- Starting valuations: 15-25 P/E
Lower the valuations higher the upside.

- Revenue growth: At least 25%
This means 2x revenue in 3 years.

- Levers for margin expansion
Operating leverage must play out.
PAT growth > REV growth.

If revenue doubles, PAT must increase by at least 3-5x.

Voila! You have found a multi bagger.

For all the stocks I add to my portfolio, I try to find the same criteria.

Observe the pattern
Do not overpay
Add only those stocks which fit in this framework
Read concalls, Investor PPT, Management Interview

One Multi bagger with right allocation can change your life.
Bookmark It

#investing #stockmarkets

- Rajeev Thakkar (PPFAS)

Never imitate the holdings.

Add 👇 if missed

items.title: Real Estate Sector - Sector for next 1-2 Years🟢 Many of the players will do extremely well🔥 Do not miss this golden opportunity of the real esta...
items.description_html:
Real Estate Sector - Sector for next 1-2 Years🟢

Many of the players will do extremely well🔥

Do not miss this golden opportunity of the real estate upcycle🌆

4 Stocks, Important Metrics, Valuations - Everything covered in detail

Bookmark 🔖 for future reference

🔸Sunteck Realty: Market Cap - 5700 Cr (CMP 390)
~ Strong foothold in MMR market.
~ Plans to double GDV in the next 3 years, 30,000 Cr --> 60,000 Cr.
~ 25% Growth, Pre-Sales Guidance of 2000 Cr in FY24.
~ Annual Rental Portfolio, Expansion from 35 Cr Current to 320 Cr by FY27.
~ Average return of rented properties 30%.
~ Rs 16,000 Cr of net surplus to be generated from 7 large projects.
~ Embedded EBITDA 35% margin.
~ Net Debt 45 Cr (0.02 D/E), Net Worth 3028 Cr.
~ Net Cash Surplus FY23 - 428 Cr, Surplus Yield - 22%.
Valuations: Mcap/Pre-Sales - 2.8 x, Ev/Embedded EBITDA - 10 x

🔸Prestige Estates: Market Cap - 47,000 Cr (CMP 1170)
~ ₹20,000 Cr Pre-Sales in FY24.
~ 15-20% Growth in FY25, Pre-Sales may exceed ₹24,000 Cr in FY25.
~ Annuity Income Portfolio of 3900 Cr by FY28.
~ Targeting ₹5,000 Cr in FY 2025, with the potential to go up to ₹10,000 Cr.
~ 32,000 Cr Top line will be recognized in the next 3 years.
~ 53 Mnsf project in the pipeline 42,000 Cr + 16,000 Cr of Inventory.
~ Aggressive Expansion in New Geographies Mumbai, Delhi NCR.
Valuations: Mcap/Pre-Sales - 2.35 x.

🔸Max Estate: Market Cap - 4050 Cr (CMP 276)
~ Best plays in Delhi NCR Region expanding its commercial & real estate portfolio.
~ 2200-2400 Cr Pre-Sales in FY25.
~ 15-20% Growth over the next 3-5 Years.
~ Targeting Sales of 4000-4500 Cr by FY29.
~ Rental Portfolio to expand to 300 Cr by FY27-28 and 400-475 Cr by FY29.
~ Plans to add 2 Mn sqft every year across NCR.
~ Estate 128 (First Luxury Residential Project) Sold out, Total Sales Booking 1800 Cr, Total Collections 441 Cr, Topline Contribution from FY28.
~ Valuations: Mcap/Pre-Sales - 2 x.

🔸Suraj Estates: Market Cap - 1150 Cr (CMP 260)
~ Suraj Estate is a South Central Mumbai-based real estate company focused on redevelopment projects.
~ The company has a legacy of 37 years and has completed 42 projects.
~ Enjoys 40% Margins for Redevelopment projects.
~ Total 29 Projects (13 - Ongoing sites, 16 - Upcoming Projects).
~ 13 Ongoing sites with a pipeline of 7.44 Lakh sqft.
~ GDV of Rs 708 Cr from ongoing projects.
~ Balance Cash flow of Rs 1,458 Cr to flow from FY25-FY29.
~ The company owns 16 land banks in Mumbai and 80% of them already have approvals.
~ 3 Launches in Q4, will exceed FY23 sales value of 635 Cr.
~ High-Cost Debt Repaid, Repaid 285 Cr Debt and 23.5 Cr additional secured debt, Interest Cost will reduce drastically. Net Debt - 219 Cr
- Mcap/Sales < 2

WHY REAL ESTATE?
- Real estate sector is in the middle of a long upcycle.

- Usually, real estate cycles are long and last for 6-8 years.

- Pre-sales growth has been solid for most of the players.

- As the interest cycle may come down in the next 6 months, the real estate sector will be the biggest beneficiary.

- Housing inventories continue to be at record low levels.

Checklist for Real Estate Companies:
- Pre-sales growth guidance of > 20%.
- Increase in Rental portfolio.
- Pipelines across various geography, Healthy Launches Pipeline.
- Leverage at monitorable levels.
- Strong Execution and Track Record.
- Embedded EBITDA Margin, Cashflows, Pre-Sales, Realisations, Collections, Inventory.
- While tracking Real Estate Stocks, Profit & Loss Statement makes no sense.
- At the end what matters the most is Cashflows, It should be monitored like a hawk.

Pre-Sales & Collections are the most important metrics to track.

VALUATIONS
Mcap/Pre-Sales is a better metric to value real estate companies.
Other Metrics - Embedded EBITDA/Mcap, OCF/Mcap

#PRESTIGE #Prestigeestates #SUNTECK #sunteckrealty #SURAJEST #surajestate #MaxEstates #MAXESTATES #RealEstate #stockmarkets #StocksToWatch

items.title: Catching up on this late — the EPFO and its processes have needed deep cleaning. This wasn’t what was needed! What was needed was easier access / be...
items.description_html:
Catching up on this late — the EPFO and its processes have needed deep cleaning.

This wasn’t what was needed! What was needed was easier access / better process to manage OUR money!

EPFO / Govt shd remember this is borrowed money. They pay us interest, yes. We lend them money, which they borrow for various purposes and more and more lately. (Borrowings from small savings).

Hope these provisions get reviewed.


Neil Borate: EPFO meeting yesterday has announced some alarming provisions:
1) Full withdrawal in case of unemployment will happen after 12 months vs 2 months currently
2) Full withdrawal of pension after 36 months rather than 2 months currently
3) 25% of your EPF, kept in EPF always!

items.description_html:
#GSTCouncil

—Common man / middle class items all taxed at 5% from 12% and 18%
—Middle class aspiration all items - TVs / ACs/ small cars / motorcycles - 18%
—Cement comes down to 18% from 28%
—Life saving drugs down to zero, including cancer drugs. Many other drugs to 5%
— 40% rate to apply to cigarettes, aerated water, carbonated beverages.
— Mid sized and large cars, aircraft for personal use at 40%
— Insurance — health and life insurance policies for individuals — exempted
— All rates applicable from Sept 22, except tobacco related products which will still have compensation cess attached *where applicable* till Covid related borrowings repaid

*Net revenue implication at Rs 48,000 crore*

items.title: India GDP growth in April-June: -Real GDP growth at a faster-than-expected 7.8%; GVA growth at 7.6% -Nominal GDP growth weak at 8.8% due to low wholes...
items.description_html:
India GDP growth in April-June:

-Real GDP growth at a faster-than-expected 7.8%; GVA growth at 7.6%
-Nominal GDP growth weak at 8.8% due to low wholesale inflation

-Big support from government spending, rising 7.4%
-Private consumption also good at 7% YoY growth on a pretty strong base

-Manufacturing strong at 7.7%, think we are seeing the impact of front-loading which continued in July-Sept quarter.

All-in-all, strong number. Yields jump a bit, thinking it makes rate cuts less likely.

https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indias-economy-grows-78-april-june-quarter-beats-forecasts-2025-08-29/

items.title: India’s food inflation has averaged about 7% over the last two years and more than 6% over the past four years. Vegetable prices have seen double dig...
items.description_html:
India’s food inflation has averaged about 7% over the last two years and more than 6% over the past four years.

Vegetable prices have seen double digit inflation for 22 months in the last four years.

Yet, the cooking cost allowance for the crucial mid day meal scheme has not been raised since Oct 2022, forcing teachers to cut back on meals given to under privileged students. Many young kids depend on this meal for their best shot at nutrition.

Reporting with Reuters’ @DashJatin and @saurabhsherry

Do read: https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indias-high-food-inflation-leaves-less-lunch-boxes-poor-school-children-2024-10-11/

- Up $87.6 billion so far this year.

- And for those with a long memory, a far cry from the $275 billion in reserves on Aug 30, 2013.

India is only the fourth economy in the world to cross $700 billion in reserves after China, Japan, and Switzerland.

Read here: https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indias-forex-reserves-top-700-bln-first-time-after-seven-week-surge-2024-10-04/

items.title: Since we whine so much about banking services, must give credit where credit is due. ⛔️Had a fraud transaction hit my CC in the middle of the night ...
items.description_html:
Since we whine so much about banking services, must give credit where credit is due.

⛔️Had a fraud transaction hit my CC in the middle of the night a couple of days back. Bank called to verify but I didn’t pick up.

⛔️Clearly bank systems detected that the transaction was atypical for me and unlikely given time of transaction and they probably had enough data to know card was not out of India.

⛔️Bank put card on temporary block, till I woke up and confirmed it was a fraudulent transaction. Transaction was reversed, new card issued when I confirmed the card transaction was fraudulent.

Thank you, unnamed bank.

items.title: If the BJP returns for a third term, as exit polls predict, manufacturing will be a big focus for the government. It’s not a new imperative for India...
items.description_html:
If the BJP returns for a third term, as exit polls predict, manufacturing will be a big focus for the government.

It’s not a new imperative for India — we’ve struggled to grow the share of manufacturing for decades and for many reasons.

At a policy level — issues with land, labour and tariffs.

At an operational level — customs processes, tax uncertainties for complex supply chains, contract enforcement.

It will take a lot to come together for India to try and rival the Chinese economic miracle, led by manufacturing.

I report, with Aftab Ahmed.

Read here: https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indias-modi-plans-post-election-reforms-rival-chinese-manufacturing-2024-06-03/

items.title: The government finally released a household consumption survey late last evening. This is the first consumption survey since 2011-12 and will also be ...
items.description_html:
The government finally released a household consumption survey late last evening. This is the first consumption survey since 2011-12 and will also be the basis of the CPI review.

Some takeaways:
- Urban consumption expectedly is up more than rural consumption.
- Monthly average rural spending has risen to Rs 3773 in 2022-23 survey from Rs 1430 in 2011-12 survey.
- Share of spending on food has declined suggesting more discretionary spend.
- Within food, share of spending on cereals is down quite sharply. More spending on processed food. This is true both in urban and rural areas.
- Within non-food, there is a jump in share of spending on durables in rural areas, conveyance, rent in urban areas.

Read here: https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indians-spending-less-food-more-discretionary-items-government-survey-2024-02-25/

items.title: Brilliant conversation between @latha_venkatesh and Finance Secretary TV Somathanan on the budget. The beginning is a mic drop moment — there is no o...
items.description_html:
Brilliant conversation between @latha_venkatesh and Finance Secretary TV Somathanan on the budget.

The beginning is a mic drop moment — there is no onus on the government to boost short term consumption, beyond taking care of the vulnerable.

We don’t have to spend because some FMCG company has reported poor quarterly sales 😐

To be clear this was said in the context of the choice between sharper cut in the fiscal deficit vs spending more

#MustWatch

items.title: UAE EASES GOLDEN VISA RULES FOR INDIANS — NO PROPERTY OR TRADE LICENSE REQUIRED Indian nationals can now apply for the UAE’s 10-year Golden Visa wit...
items.description_html:
UAE EASES GOLDEN VISA RULES FOR INDIANS — NO PROPERTY OR TRADE LICENSE REQUIRED

Indian nationals can now apply for the UAE’s 10-year Golden Visa without needing to invest in property or hold a trade license.

The updated policy removes the previous requirement of a minimum AED 2 million (approx ₹4.7 crore) property investment or a qualifying business license.

This major relaxation broadens access to long-term residency for Indian professionals, entrepreneurs, investors, and skilled workers—one of the UAE’s largest expatriate groups.

items.title: Attention @indianrailway__ @RailMinIndia: My experience at Howrah Duranto 12261. Booked 1st class ticket, paying almost same amount we pay for a fligh...
items.description_html: Attention @indianrailway__ @RailMinIndia: My experience at Howrah Duranto 12261. Booked 1st class ticket, paying almost same amount we pay for a flight. Started late from CST. Till Bilaspur, ran 1 hour late. From there, it crawled to Tatanagar, 6 hours behind schedule. At 20.20 it should be at Howrah. At 20.50, I’m eating khichri for dinner after crossing Tatanagar. Railway employees call it “Khichri Train”. This happens everyday for many years now. Will wait for the Bullet Train.

items.title: With Groww’s listing, Silicon Valley’s Y Combinator has cashed out $120 million — its first IPO exit in India! Y Combinator has backed over 200 sta...
items.description_html:
With Groww’s listing, Silicon Valley’s Y Combinator has cashed out $120 million — its first IPO exit in India!

Y Combinator has backed over 200 startups in India, with most of the deals between 2014 and 2022, typically involving investments of $120,000–150,000 each.

That amounts to roughly $32 million invested at the upper end in its seed portfolio.

Even assuming twice that amount was spent on follow-on rounds, the cashout at Groww’s IPO alone would be enough to return its total India investment.

Y Combinator still holds close to a 10% stake post-IPO, valued at over $900 million. Growth fund holds another 1.2% stake.

And its other portfolio companies, like Meesho, Zepto, and Razorpay, still have to go for an IPO.

items.description_html:
Groww’s 1,400+ employees have created Rs 44 crore each in value at the IPO market capitalisation.

2× more than any other listed Indian internet company.

When discussing internet platforms, we often refer to network effects, margins, and operating leverage.

But one under-discussed indicator of a platform’s strength, in my view, is how much leverage each employee creates.

Groww runs one of the leanest teams among large venture-backed companies going for IPO, which is what makes it interesting.

The shares were acquired at Rs 402 a piece, which also appears to be the upper end of Lenskart’s IPO price band.

items.title: Reliance Retail is finally going all-in on quick commerce. - 600 dark stores added in the last two quarters to complement the 3,000 retail stores it w...
items.description_html:
Reliance Retail is finally going all-in on quick commerce.

- 600 dark stores added in the last two quarters to complement the 3,000 retail stores it was already delivering from.

- Jiomart has pivoted from next-day to 30-minute delivery

“We were a bit late… but we’ve re-pivoted completely,” said CFO Dinesh Taluja on the earnings call.

It now wants to take market share from incumbents like Blinkit, Swiggy and Zepto in the metros.

Reliance says its quick commerce offering is now in 1,000 cities vs 152 cities for market leader Blinkit.

It wants to fortify its presence in smaller towns using the network of Reliance Fresh stores, where competition is not present.

More from the earnings call 👇

items.description_html:

CoD still accounts for over 75% of Meesho’s orders, which have a lower success rate as compared to prepaid orders (75% vs 96%)

This can mean a visit to the police station 👮

“We have had to file complaints against delivery vendors not depositing cash collected. For example, on May 15, 2024, our Company filed a complaint before the Marathahalli Police Station, Bengaluru, against 35 delivery vendors for allegedly failing to deposit the
CoD amounts collected by them with us, for which an FIR is yet to be registered.”

items.title: 🚨Early Access To Exclusive Swiggy Report🚨 Swiggy is going for IPO at Rs 95,000 cr valuation - 8.5 times its trailing FY24 revenue. Rival Zomato ...
items.description_html:
🚨Early Access To Exclusive Swiggy Report🚨

Swiggy is going for IPO at Rs 95,000 cr valuation - 8.5 times its trailing FY24 revenue.

Rival Zomato has a market cap of Rs 218,257 cr - 18 times its trailing FY24 revenue.

So, Swiggy is giving a 50% valuation multiple discount.

Why❓

Swiggy won the first leg of the race for food and quick deliveries between 2015 and 2021, but now Zomato holds the top position in both segments.

More importantly, Zomato has also been able to reach profitability and break-even across both these segments.

This raises important questions about Swiggy’s future. If Swiggy is to outpace Zomato in the coming decade, incremental gains in market share or marginal growth will not be enough.

The company will need to deliver new products that significantly expand its total addressable market (TAM).

While a publicly listed Zomato chases aggressive targets, Swiggy continues to move carefully in every area, whether monetisation or cost-cutting.

All of this goes back to the very different DNAs of the two companies, which are driven by their founders.

In this extensively researched report, @thearc_hq provides an exclusive profile of Swiggy’s reclusive founder, Sriharsha Majety, based on over a dozen interviews.

We also explore how Swiggy’s top management team is structured, how its product strategy differs from Zomato’s, and what the future may hold.

Link - https://docsend.com/v/7v8z5/swiggyfilesbythearctm

items.title: 🚨 Nandan Nilekani says that India’s path in AI (artificial intelligence) is different. Indian startups have to focus on applications and use cases...
items.description_html:
🚨 Nandan Nilekani says that India’s path in AI (artificial intelligence) is different.

Indian startups have to focus on applications and use cases rather than building the biggest large language models (LLMs) like Silicon Valley startups.

“Let those people with capital, and who have better chips do that,” he said.

“Our advantage currently lies not in compute, cloud, or chips. 𝐎𝐮𝐫 𝐚𝐝𝐯𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐠𝐞 𝐢𝐬 𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐩𝐨𝐩𝐮𝐥𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐢𝐫 𝐚𝐬𝐩𝐢𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬. 𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐢𝐬 𝐰𝐡𝐲 𝐰𝐞 𝐡𝐚𝐯𝐞 𝐭𝐨 𝐛𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐝𝐨𝐰𝐧 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐬𝐭 𝐨𝐟 𝐢𝐧𝐟𝐞𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐑𝐬 100 𝐭𝐨 𝐑𝐬 1……𝐈𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐟𝐫𝐮𝐠𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲 𝐭𝐨 𝐝𝐫𝐚𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲 𝐫𝐞𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐬𝐭 𝐨𝐟 𝐀𝐈. 𝐖𝐞 𝐜𝐚𝐧'𝐭 𝐝𝐞𝐥𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐫 𝐢𝐭 𝐭𝐨 𝐚 𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐩𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐥𝐞 𝐮𝐧𝐥𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐰𝐞 𝐜𝐚𝐧 𝐛𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐭𝐨 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐞 1 𝐫𝐮𝐩𝐞𝐞 𝐩𝐞𝐫 𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧.”

"Winners in AI in India will be those who meet customers where they are," Nilekani said at an event by @PeoplePlusAI earlier this week. These are some comments that I found interesting from his chat.

Startups and companies should leverage AI’s capabilities to personalize products at scale, which has not been possible earlier, according to Nilekani.

“We are moving to a world where instead of you adjusting to the technology, the technology will adjust to you. It will adjust to you as a person, adjust to your skills, adjust your talents, adjust, your capabilities, adjust your language, and your skills……what we have found is whenever we make it easier for technology to adjust to you then our usage goes up,” he said.

Nilekani mentioned that companies should leverage the combination of digital public infrastructure (DPI) and AI. DPI is the combination of Aadhaar, UPI, Account Aggregator (AA), and ONDC open-source software.

This includes examples like leveraging local language capabilities of AI platforms like Bhashini and Sarvam to deliver personalized financial services products or offers, by understanding customer's banking profiles through AA.

There was a demo by Pine Labs/Setu of a new chat-based personal finance product.

“We have a unique infrastructure in India where we are building digital public infrastructure for AI. We are building a set of digital public goods for AI. We are building on top of an existing DPI to power AI and therefore accelerating the adoption of AI in India,” said Nilekani.

“And that's what this is a really the unique way that India is doing in AI which is what gives us confidence that we can move very rapidly in this space. Now, we talk about unbundling, but now with AI, we have a chance to bundle again. It was James Clarke of Netscape who said that we spend half our lives unbundling and then the next half re-bundling.”

“This means that all these AI Tools now allow you to put things back together in a very effective way to get the full impact of the solutions. Because for the first time, we can now personalize at scale.”

items.title: Good morning Twitter. Remember that an annualized rate means the economy would be growing by 33.1% if it was growing at the same rate for four quarter...
items.description_html:
Good morning Twitter. Remember that an annualized rate means the economy would be growing by 33.1% if it was growing at the same rate for four quarters.

But since that's not happening, the 2020 Q3 economy grew by about 7.4% compared to 2020 Q2.


BEA News: The U.S. economy grew at an annual rate of 33.1% in Q3. https://go.usa.gov/xUSSS

Josh Hawley: THREAD/ A few points in response. 1. My basic claim is that to confront #China economic imperialism & protect American security & Econ interests, the international system must be reformed. That includes the global economic system.

items.title: Trump HAS gotten China to lower its tariffs...on everyone else. China has increased tariffs on US exports to an average 20.7%. But China has also *red...
items.description_html: Trump HAS gotten China to lower its tariffs...on everyone else. China has increased tariffs on US exports to an average 20.7%. But China has also *reduced* tariffs on competing products imported from everyone else to an average of only 6.7%. https://piie.com/blogs/trade-investment-policy-watch/trump-has-gotten-china-lower-its-tariffs-just-toward-everyone
